name: excited
description: Measures excited/enthusiastic vs bored/indifferent emotional tone in language

pairs.positive: I can barely sit still thinking about the trip we're taking next week.
pairs.negative: The trip next week is just another thing on the calendar I have to get through.

pairs.positive: The new project at work has me buzzing with ideas and I can't wait to start.
pairs.negative: The new project at work is just more of the same and I couldn't care less.

pairs.positive: I tore open the package the moment it arrived, grinning the whole time.
pairs.negative: The package arrived and I left it on the counter for a few days before opening it.

pairs.positive: The concert lineup was announced and I immediately started planning my outfit.
pairs.negative: The concert lineup was announced and I scrolled past it without a second thought.

pairs.positive: I stayed up all night reading because I couldn't put the book down.
pairs.negative: I tried reading but lost interest after a few pages and put it aside.

pairs.positive: The first snowfall of the year made me rush outside like a kid.
pairs.negative: The first snowfall of the year was just another weather event to deal with.

pairs.positive: I'm thrilled about learning to surf and already booked three lessons.
pairs.negative: They suggested I try surfing but it sounded tedious so I passed.

pairs.positive: The trailer for the new film gave me chills and I've already watched it five times.
pairs.negative: The trailer for the new film looked generic and I didn't bother finishing it.

pairs.positive: I practically ran to the kitchen when I smelled the cookies coming out of the oven.
pairs.negative: The cookies came out of the oven and I figured I'd have one later, maybe.

pairs.positive: Meeting the author of my favorite book in person was electrifying.
pairs.negative: I met the author at a signing but it felt routine and forgettable.

pairs.positive: The sunrise from the mountain summit was so spectacular I shouted out loud.
pairs.negative: The sunrise from the summit was fine, about what you'd expect.

pairs.positive: I've been counting down the days until the new season of the show premieres.
pairs.negative: The new season of the show is coming out but I'm not sure I'll bother watching.

pairs.positive: The idea of starting my own business fills me with adrenaline and possibility.
pairs.negative: Starting a business sounds like a lot of effort for something that probably won't work.

pairs.positive: I jumped up and cheered when my team scored in the final seconds.
pairs.negative: My team scored but I barely looked up from my phone.

pairs.positive: The invitation to the exclusive event made my heart race with anticipation.
pairs.negative: I got invited to some event but it didn't really register as important.

pairs.positive: I rearranged my entire weekend to make time for the food festival downtown.
pairs.negative: There's a food festival downtown but I'd rather just stay home and do nothing.

pairs.positive: The new software update has features I've been hoping for and I'm eager to try them.
pairs.negative: The software updated again and I didn't look at what changed.

pairs.positive: I woke up on my birthday feeling like the day was full of possibility.
pairs.negative: My birthday came and went and it felt like any other day.

pairs.positive: The dance floor was calling and I pulled my friends in with pure enthusiasm.
pairs.negative: Everyone was dancing but I stood against the wall, completely uninterested.

pairs.positive: I signed up for the marathon and have been energized by every training run.
pairs.negative: Someone suggested I run a marathon but the whole idea bores me.

pairs.positive: Discovering a new band that matches my taste perfectly got me listening on repeat.
pairs.negative: Someone recommended a new band but it all sounded the same to me.

pairs.positive: The science museum exhibit was so fascinating I lost track of time completely.
pairs.negative: The science museum was fine but I was ready to leave after twenty minutes.

pairs.positive: I was so pumped after the workout that I added an extra set just for fun.
pairs.negative: I went through the motions at the gym and left as soon as I could.

pairs.positive: The chance to travel somewhere I've never been makes me feel completely alive.
pairs.negative: Traveling to a new place sounds exhausting and I'd rather stay put.

pairs.positive: I grabbed my camera and ran outside when the double rainbow appeared.
pairs.negative: Someone mentioned there was a rainbow but I didn't bother looking.

pairs.positive: The puzzle was so engaging I worked on it for four hours straight without noticing.
pairs.negative: I tried the puzzle but gave up after ten minutes out of sheer boredom.

pairs.positive: Getting accepted into the program filled me with a surge of motivation.
pairs.negative: I got into the program but it didn't change how I feel about anything.

pairs.positive: The farmers market had so many new vendors I wanted to try everything.
pairs.negative: The farmers market had the usual stalls and nothing caught my eye.

pairs.positive: I texted everyone I know when I found out the good news because I had to share it.
pairs.negative: I got some good news but didn't feel the need to tell anyone about it.

pairs.positive: The new recipe turned out so well I'm already planning to make it again tomorrow.
pairs.negative: The recipe turned out fine but I don't see myself making it again.
